summary: PR 2745 reorganized the game-mode tournament-related code/data split by deleting the large `gm_18A5.c` unit and old `gmtou` headers, adding `gmtoulib.c`, `gmtou_0.c`, `gmtou_1.c`, and adjusted `gmtou_2.c`, and updating split/config/symbol metadata. The bot report shows net code and data match improvements, especially data, while many old-unit matches appear as broken because functions/data moved into new units.

key_files.role: Updated split ranges, replacing `gm_18A5.c` and `gmtou.c` with `gmtoulib.c`, `gmtou_0.c`, `gmtou_1.c`, and `gmtou_2.c` section ranges.
Adjusted data symbol sizes, scopes, and data kinds for the moved tournament-mode data region.
Updated object list to build `gmtoulib`, `gmtou_0`, `gmtou_1`, and `gmtou_2` instead of old `gm_18A5` and `gmtou` objects.
Deleted old large source unit as part of the split reorganization.
Added new tournament-library source unit covering the early split range starting at 0x8018A514.
Added new tournament-mode source unit covering 0x80190ABC-0x80196510.
Added new tournament-mode source unit covering 0x80196510-0x8019BA08.
Adjusted existing/renamed tournament-mode source unit to cover 0x8019BA08-0x8019EFC4 with data and bss range changes.
Expanded game-mode type definitions used by the split/recovered code.
Updated public declarations for the new `gmtoulib` unit.
Added static declarations for `gmtoulib`.
Added declarations for `gmtou_1`.
Added declarations for `gmtou_2`.
Deleted obsolete tournament header content.
Deleted obsolete tournament static header content.
Deleted old documentation/prototype extraction for the removed unit.
Deleted static declarations for the removed `gm_18A5` unit.
Replaced placeholder struct access in `fn_80166CBC` with `MatchEnd` and named field access.
Added extracted declarations/documentation for many `gm_1601` functions.
Reordered and expanded forward declarations needed by recovered types and headers.
Special-cased `STATIC_ASSERT` under `M2CTX` to expand empty.
Updated game-mode declarations related to the type/signature cleanup.
Removed static declarations superseded by new header/dox organization.
Small include/declaration or callsite adjustment associated with header split cleanup.
Small declaration update associated with header split cleanup.
One-line source adjustment caused by declaration/header changes.
One-line source adjustment caused by declaration/header changes.
One-line source adjustment caused by declaration/header changes.
Small declaration adjustment tied to the game-mode split changes.
One-line source adjustment caused by declaration/header changes.
One-line source adjustment caused by declaration/header changes.
One-line source adjustment caused by declaration/header changes.
One-line source adjustment caused by declaration/header changes.
One-line source adjustment caused by declaration/header changes.
One-line interface source adjustment associated with type/header changes.
One-line menu source adjustment associated with type/header changes.
One-line menu source adjustment associated with type/header changes.
Added documentation/prototype extraction for `gmtoulib`.

what_changed: Replaced split entries for `melee/gm/gm_18A5.c` and `melee/gm/gmtou.c` with four split units: `gmtoulib.c`, `gmtou_0.c`, `gmtou_1.c`, and `gmtou_2.c`.
Updated `configure.py` so the build object list follows the new `gmtoulib`, `gmtou_0`, `gmtou_1`, and `gmtou_2` split layout.
Deleted old `gm_18A5.c`, `gm_18A5.dox`, `gm_18A5.static.h`, `gmtou.h`, and `gmtou.static.h` content while adding new source/header/static-header/dox files for the split units.

Adjusted symbol metadata around `.data`, `.bss`, and `.sdata2`, including local scope annotations, byte/string data kinds, and corrected sizes for labels such as `lbl_803D9EE8`, `lbl_803D9F80`, `lbl_803DA0D0`, `gm_80477738`, and `lbl_804DA948`.
Improved type usage in `gm_1601.c`: `fn_80166CBC` now takes `MatchEnd*` and reads `arg0->player_standings[index].is_small_loser` instead of indexing a placeholder struct and raw field `x5E`.

Added or updated forward declarations and headers for recovered game-mode structures such as `MatchEnd`, `MatchExitInfo`, `MenuEnterData`, `MenuExitData`, `ResultsData`, `TmAnimTimers`, and related tournament/game-mode types.
Changed `STATIC_ASSERT` behavior in `src/Runtime/platform.h` so `M2CTX` defines `STATIC_ASSERT(cond)` as empty before the MWERKS implementation.

Bot report recorded `Matched code: 75.41% (+0.03%, +1064 bytes)` and `Matched data: 81.05% (+2.10%, +25416 bytes)`, with 115 new matches, 104 broken matches, 55 unmatched-item improvements, and 59 unmatched-item regressions.

smart_moves.lesson: When a large nonmatching unit is actually a bad split, update splits, object lists, headers, and symbol metadata together rather than only moving source code.

Treat apparent match breaks after a split with caution: the bot report shows many old `gm_18A5`/`gmtou` matches broken while corresponding `gmtoulib`/`gmtou_0`/`gmtou_1`/`gmtou_2` items become new matches.
Data match work may require tightening symbol metadata, not only adding C definitions; this PR adjusted scope, size, and data-kind annotations for several labels.
Use recovered aggregate types when available: the `fn_80166CBC` change demonstrates replacing placeholder member access with a `MatchEnd` field chain.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Split-boundary PRs can produce noisy before/after reports where old-unit matches become broken and new-unit matches appear; compare by address/function identity and unit movement before treating the broken list as true regressions.
When moving a large data-bearing game-mode unit, keep split section ranges and symbol metadata synchronized across `.text`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2`.

Recovered type fields should replace placeholder struct offsets when a surrounding type is known; `MatchEnd.player_standings[index].is_small_loser` is more reviewable than `struct fn_80166CBC_arg0_t[index].x5E`.

naming_conventions.convention: New split files use the observed `gmtoulib`, `gmtou_0`, `gmtou_1`, `gmtou_2` naming scheme matching split ranges and configure object names.

Unproven function names remain address-style `fn_801...`/`gm_801...`, while recovered struct and field names such as `MatchEnd`, `player_standings`, and `is_small_loser` are used where supported by types.
Data symbols retained address-style `lbl_...`/`gm_...` names while metadata changed scope/size/type, avoiding unsupported semantic renames in the shown symbol diff.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Perform split surgery by moving section boundaries first: old `gm_18A5` text range 0x8018A514-0x8019B8C4 and old `gmtou` range 0x8019B8C4-0x8019EFC4 were redistributed into `gmtoulib`, `gmtou_0`, `gmtou_1`, and `gmtou_2` ranges.
Update build configuration in lockstep with split changes so objdiff/build reports track the new unit names.
Use symbol metadata corrections for local scope and byte/string classification to improve data matching without inventing semantic symbol names.
Make `M2CTX` tolerate `STATIC_ASSERT` by eliding it in `platform.h`; evidence only shows the preprocessor change, not the author’s broader intent.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Automated report showed net improvement: matched code +1064 bytes and matched data +25416 bytes, with many new matches in the new `gmtou*` units and many broken matches in the old removed units.
No human review comments were included in the loaded PR slice; reviewer intent or requested changes cannot be inferred.

notes: Strong evidence exists for file list, split/config changes, symbol metadata changes, the `gm_1601.c` typed-field edit, and automated match deltas.
The PR body is empty and there are no loaded human review comments, so reviewer intent and rationale beyond title/diff/bot output are not supported.

The raw diff is very large and truncated in the loaded slice; detailed source-level tactics inside newly added `gmtoulib.c`, `gmtou_0.c`, and `gmtou_1.c` cannot be fully characterized from this intake packet.

Bot report shows many broken matches and regressions, but the corresponding unit migration strongly suggests much of the noise is caused by split renaming/movement; no independent validation was run during intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret match reports carefully after split-boundary PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a PR changes split boundaries and object names, automated reports may show old-unit matches as broken while corresponding new-unit matches appear. Compare by moved addresses/functions and split metadata before classifying these as real regressions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer recovered aggregate fields over placeholder structs
accepted_candidate_records.text: `fn_80166CBC` changed from a placeholder struct/index/raw member access to `MatchEnd*` and `player_standings[index].is_small_loser`, matching the typed-fields standard and improving reviewability.

source_update_candidates.title: gmtou split layout after PR 2745
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2745 replaced old `melee/gm/gm_18A5.c` and `melee/gm/gmtou.c` split entries with `melee/gm/gmtoulib.c` (text 0x8018A514-0x80190ABC), `melee/gm/gmtou_0.c` (0x80190ABC-0x80196510), `melee/gm/gmtou_1.c` (0x80196510-0x8019BA08), and `melee/gm/gmtou_2.c` (0x8019BA08-0x8019EFC4). Curator should verify against current source before promoting.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped context for future gmtou/gmtoulib work, but source-owned split facts should be checked against current splits before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1601 typed access for fn_80166CBC
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2745 changed `fn_80166CBC` to accept `MatchEnd*` and return `arg0->player_standings[index].is_small_loser` instead of using `struct fn_80166CBC_arg0_t*` and `x5E`.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future repair work around `MatchEnd` and player standings fields; verify current code before adding a path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate M2CTX STATIC_ASSERT handling
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2745 added an `#ifdef M2CTX` branch in `src/Runtime/platform.h` that defines `STATIC_ASSERT(cond)` empty before the MWERKS implementation. The slice does not explain whether this should be a general m2ctx standard or only a local compatibility fix.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential tooling/context-generation convention, but author rationale is absent, so it should not be promoted without curator verification.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all new `gmtou*` source bodies are fully matched; bot report lists many 100% new matches but also many partial improvements and unmatched regressions.
Do not treat the bot report's broken old-unit matches as ordinary regressions without accounting for the split rename/migration.
Do not promote `STATIC_ASSERT` elision under `M2CTX` as a global standard from this PR alone; the diff shows the change but not the rationale.
Do not infer human reviewer approval criteria or requested changes; no human review comments were loaded.
